channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Company recalls 2 million pounds of barbecue pork jerky that may contain metal wire
item.description: (The Associated Press) -- A South Dakota company is recalling more than 2.2 million pounds (998,000 kilograms) of Korean barbecue pork jerky sold at Costco and Sam's Club stores because the product may be contaminated with pieces of metal, federal health officials said Friday. LSI, Inc.
item.dc:creator: Jonel Aleccia, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: By the Numbers: Why trick-or-treaters may bag more gummy candy than chocolate this Halloween
item.description: Ghouls, goblins … and gummy bears. Trick-or-treaters may find more fruity candy than chocolate among their Halloween handouts this year.
item.dc:creator: DEE-ANN DURBIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Shutdowns began as a way to enforce federal law. Now Trump is using it to take more power
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The government shutdown, already the second-longest in history, with no end in sight, is quickly becoming a way for President Donald Trump to exercise new command over the government. It wasn't always this way.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: What is 'ghost tapping?' New scheme targeting tap-to-pay users
item.description: The Better Business Bureau is warning tap-to-pay users of a new scheme that could make simple transactions costly.
item.dc:creator: Katlyn Fernandez, NewsNation

item.title: Ontario premier says he'll pull ad that upset Trump so trade talks between Canada and US can resume
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — The leader of Canada’s most populous province said Friday he’ll pull the anti-tariff ad that prompted U.S. President Donald Trump to end trade talks with Canada.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Michigan babysitter accused of sexually abusing children in her care faces 47 charges, police say
item.description: Michigan babysitter Gaila Bennett was arrested and faces 47 counts of criminal sexual misconduct for allegedly abusing children in her Midland home.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 40% of workers making $300K-plus say they live paycheck to paycheck
item.description: The two groups most likely to say they're living paycheck to paycheck? Those making under $50k — and over $300K.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: Security video captures moment former mayor stabbed multiple times outside his blue state business
item.description: Security video shows the moment a former Massachusetts mayor was stabbed on Monday afternoon outside his cannabis dispensary, leaving him hospitalized.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: In emotional speech, Zohran Mamdani defends Muslim identity against 'racist and baseless' attacks
item.description: Zohran Mamdani, the Democratic nominee for New York City mayor, is pledging to embrace his Muslim identity amid growing attacks in the final days of the campaign.
item.dc:creator: Jake Offenhartz, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: US expands facial recognition at border, airports
item.description: Facial recognition technology will be expanded for U.S. border authorities to require non-citizens to be photographed at airports, seaports, and border land crossings.
item.dc:creator: Ted Hesson, NewsNation

item.title: Body found during search for missing Vermont college student Lia Smith near Middlebury campus
item.description: Body discovered during search for missing Vermont college student Lia Smith, 21, who vanished from Middlebury College campus. Autopsy scheduled to determine identity.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Latest: Trump to meet with Asian leaders
item.description: President Donald Trump is going to Japan and South Korea next week to promote an epic financial windfall — at least $900 billion in investments for U.S. factories, a natural gas pipeline and other projects.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Hollywood piano teacher allegedly flees country after sexual abuse conviction involving teen
item.description: Hollywood piano teacher John Kaleel convicted of sexually abusing teen client allegedly fled country before sentencing, facing up to 10 years in prison.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Alaska Airlines operations resume after ground stop over IT outage
item.description: Alaska Airlines said its operations have resumed Friday after it issued a temporary ground stoppage Thursday evening amid an IT outage.
item.dc:creator: Max Rego, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Alaska Airlines operations resume after ground stop over IT outage
item.description: Alaska Airlines said its operations have resumed Friday after it issued a temporary ground stoppage Thursday evening amid an IT outage.
item.dc:creator: Max Rego, KRON4

item.title: NBA head coach and player charged in sprawling sports betting and Mafia-backed poker schemes
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The head coach of the Portland Trail Blazers and a player for the Miami Heat were arrested Thursday along with more than 30 other people in a takedown of two sprawling gambling operations that authorities said leaked inside information about NBA athletes and rigged poker games backed
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, TIM REYNOLDS and PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Alabama executes man with nitrogen gas for 1993 murder over $200 drug debt
item.description: ATMORE, Ala. (AP) — An Alabama man convicted of helping to burn a man alive in 1993 over a $200 drug debt was executed by nitrogen gas on Thursday. Anthony Boyd, 54, was pronounced dead at 6:33 p.m. at William C. Holman Correctional Facility, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: KIM CHANDLER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Virginia Democrats will try to reshape US House districts in counter to Trump's redistricting push
item.description: RICHMOND, Va. (AP) — Virginia Democrats are taking steps to redraw their state’s U.S. House districts, hoping to boost their party’s chances in next year’s midterm elections and counter President Donald Trump’s push for more partisan districts in Republican-run states.
item.dc:creator: OLIVIA DIAZ and DAVID A. LIEB, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Bodycam footage of Jay Cutler's arrest released
item.description: Cutler, 41, was arrested in October 2024 after police responded to a minor traffic collision where he had rear-ended another vehicle.
item.dc:creator: Brittney Baird, Fox 5

item.title: Nationwide auto theft ring leads to Bay Area car business: CA AG
item.description: (KRON) -- Forty felony charges were filed Thursday against eight people tied to a nationwide auto theft ring and "complex" altered vehicle identification number scheme. The suspects then sold the 19 stolen cars out of their Bay Area business, announced California Attorney General Rob Bonta.
item.dc:creator: Tor Smith, KRON4

item.title: Deadly semitrailer crash in California renews federal criticism of immigrant truck drivers
item.description: A 21-year-old semitruck driver accused of being under the influence of drugs and causing a fiery crash that killed three people on a southern California freeway is in the country illegally, U.S. Homeland Security officials said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: COREY WILLIAMS, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump backs off planned surge of federal agents into San Francisco after talking to the mayor
item.description: ALAMEDA, Calif. (AP) — President Donald Trump said Thursday that he’s backing off a planned surge of federal agents into San Francisco to quell crime after speaking to the mayor and several prominent business leaders who said they're working hard to clean up the city.
item.dc:creator: TERRY CHEA and CHRISTOPHER WEBER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: As federal agents ramp up Chicago immigration crackdown, more elected officials caught in crosshairs
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Hoan Huynh was going door-to-door informing businesses of ramped up immigration enforcement on Chicago’s North Side when the Democratic state lawmaker got an activist notification of federal agents nearby.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Florida crocodile found with spear, bullet in its head; Zoo works to save her
item.description: An 8-foot American crocodile is on her way to recovery after what appears to have been a harrowing ordeal.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Family of missing Kentucky teen asks for her safe return, offers cash reward
item.description: After more than a week of searching and distributing flyers, the family is wondering why more hasn't been done.
item.dc:creator: Daegiona Wilson, NewsNation

item.title: 4 family members of Illinois governor candidate killed in Montana helicopter crash, campaign says
item.description: Four family members of a Republican running for governor in Illinois were killed in a Montana helicopter crash, his campaign said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: JOHN SEEWER and CHRISTINE FERNANDO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump calls off plans to deploy federal troops to San Francisco
item.description: The president said Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang and Salesforce CEO Marc Benioff were among "great people" who urged him not to deploy the National Guard to the city.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, Fox 5

item.title: Cracker Barrel CEO explains reason for attempt to change logo: report
item.description: Cracker Barrel had debuted its new logo in August, but quickly faced backlash for ditching a depiction of the “Old Timer” previously seen on the sign.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: New US citizenship test proving difficult for immigrants
item.description: A new citizenship test that was rolled out this week by the Trump administration is being criticized as too difficult and could deter people from becoming U.S. citizens.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, ABC 4

item.title: US Marshal, suspect struck by gunfire during LA immigration enforcement operation: officials
item.description: Federal authorities said the man "weaponized his vehicle," prompting immigration agents to fire "defensive shots.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, Fox 5

item.title: Things to know about Nicolas Sarkozy, France's new prison inmate who used to be president
item.description: PARIS (AP) — Once the most powerful person in France, Nicolas Sarkozy is now behind bars. Being locked up in Paris' La Santé prison for criminal conspiracy is the latest twist in the uncommon life of the 70-year-old former president.
item.dc:creator: JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Former NFL player who died in police custody battled injuries and mental health challenges
item.description: Doug Martin arrived on the campus of Boise State University nearly two decades ago to play football on its famed blue turf and, despite being a squat, little-known recruit from Northern California, turned himself into a hard-nosed running back and a first-round NFL draft pick.
item.dc:creator: JOHN SEEWER and MARK LONG, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A United Airlines emergency landing likely caused by collision with a weather balloon
item.description: A United Airlines plane appears to have collided with a weather balloon while cruising over Utah at 36,000 feet last week, fracturing layers of its windshield and forcing an emergency landing.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The Latest: Senate Republicans gathering at White House as shutdown effects worsen
item.description: As the government shutdown enters its fourth week, Senate Republicans arrived at the White House on Tuesday — not for urgent talks on how to end it, but for a display of unity with President Donald Trump as they refuse to negotiate on any Democratic demands.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Man who planned to shoot up Atlanta's airport is arrested in a terminal following a tip, police say
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Police arrested a man at Atlanta's bustling airport on Monday after getting a tip from his family that he was planning to shoot up the place, and found an assault rifle and ammunition in his truck outside, the city's police chief said.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN and KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: France's former President Sarkozy will begin serving a 5-year prison sentence Tuesday
item.description: Nicolas Sarkozy will become the first former French president in living memory to be imprisoned when he is expected to begin a five-year sentence Tuesday in Paris’ La Santé prison.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY SCHAEFFER and NICOLAS GARRIGA, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Kevin Federline says his sons with Britney Spears are the reason for his new memoir
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Kevin Federline says concern for his two sons with Britney Spears long kept him from telling his story, and those same concerns are the reason he's telling it now that they're men.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DALTON, Associated Press, Fox 5
